summary: Backend regression testing after the modular refactor (server.py → routes/* + services/*). 17/17 pytest cases passed. All endpoints behave identically to pre-refactor and Pydantic validations still trigger 422 for invalid contribuição values. Auto-sync of valores_mensais on POST/DELETE /api/contribuicoes confirmed working. Dizimistas count == 156 as expected.
passed_tests: GET /api/auth/me with token -> 200, returns admin user
GET /api/users (admin) -> 200, list contains admin, no _id and no password leaked
GET /api/dizimistas -> 200, returns 156 records, no _id, has id+nome
POST /api/contribuicoes valid -> 200, auto-sync creates entry in valores_mensais (mes=1, ano=2026), DELETE removes it
GET /api/relatorios/resumo -> 200 with all required keys (total_dizimistas, dizimistas_ativos, dizimistas_pendentes, dizimistas_inativos, total_arrecadado, por_mes)
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py: jwt.JWTError import path - works because PyJWT exposes it, but jwt.InvalidTokenError is the canonical exception. Not breaking, just style.
server.py: uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); consider migrating to lifespan handlers (no functional impact).
server.py: imports `APIRouter`, `uuid`, `datetime` and `Path` not strictly needed at top scope after refactor — only `uuid`/`datetime`/`hash_password` are used inside startup. Minor cleanup.

routes/dizimistas.py POST /import/excel does not call atomic insert_many and silently inserts even when parse_workbook returns errors — consider partial rollback or transaction wrapping (pre-existing behavior, not introduced by refactor).
auth.py SECRET_KEY has a default fallback string; for production hygiene it should fail fast if JWT_SECRET is missing (low priority).

test_credentials.note: /app/memory/test_credentials.md is missing — credentials were taken from review_request and verified working.

seed_data_creation: One transient contribuição (valor=12.34, mes_referencia=1, data=2026-01-15) was created and then deleted within the same test. No residual test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor is functionally clean. server.py is a slim entry point (83 lines), routes are split per-resource, services hold excel/sync logic. All API paths still under /api/* prefix. Pydantic validations preserved. Frontend was explicitly out of scope for this run. If frontend tested next, verify token storage location: should be sessionStorage under key 'psjt.auth.token' (not localStorage) per the XSS-mitigation requirement.
